description: Confirm and pin POST /api/assessments as the amend mechanism. Creating an assessment for a pitch that already has one appends a NEW row with version = previous latest + 1; it never mutates or overwrites a prior version. assessor_id is set to the acting user and assessment_date is taken from the request. Criteria scores are validated to the 1-5 range. admin+assessor may create; viewer 403; all authenticated roles may list/read. There is no PATCH/PUT/DELETE for assessments (versioning is the only amendment path).
expectedBehavior: POST for a pitch with an existing assessment returns 200 with version = previous latest + 1; the prior row is unchanged on a subsequent GET
The created version's assessor_id is the acting user and assessment_date is the supplied date
A score outside 1-5 (e.g. 0 or 6) returns 422 and creates no new version
viewer POST -> 403; unauthenticated -> 401; GET list/detail -> 200 for admin/assessor/viewer
No update or delete endpoint exists for assessments
verificationSteps: cd backend && pytest tests/test_assessments.py -k 'version or create or rbac or validation'

description: On AssessmentDetailPage (and/or the pitch view), add an 'Amend' / 'New version' affordance (admin+assessor only) that opens the assessment form pre-filled from the current latest version, and on save POSTs to /api/assessments to create the next version, then shows the new version. Render the full version history (newest first) with the latest marked as current and every prior version openable and read-only. Hide the amend control for viewers.
expectedBehavior: Detail view shows an amend/new-version control for admin/assessor and hides it for viewer
The amend form is pre-filled from the current latest version; saving calls api.post('/assessments', ...) and shows the incremented version
The version-history list shows all versions newest-first with the current one marked; prior versions open read-only
Cancelling the amend form makes no api.post call

description: Fix SearchPage result navigation. The /api/search response tags each result with a singular type ('pitch', 'organisation', 'contact', 'meeting', 'assessment'); the click handler currently mis-maps the pitch type to an empty basePath, so selecting a pitch navigates to '/' (dashboard). Map each result type to its correct detail route (pitch -> /pitches/{id}, organisation -> /organisations/{id}, contact -> /contacts/{id}, meeting -> /meetings/{id}, assessment -> /assessments/{id}) so no result type falls through to the dashboard.
expectedBehavior: Clicking a pitch search result navigates to /pitches/{id} and renders that pitch, not the dashboard
Clicking a result of any other type navigates to that type's detail route
No result type resolves to an empty basePath or the catch-all/dashboard route

description: Confirm and pin PATCH /api/contacts/{id} as a partial update over ContactUpdate. Only supplied fields change; omitted fields are preserved. admin+assessor allowed; viewer 403; unauthenticated 401; unknown id 404. ContactUpdate stays an explicit allowlist (name, role, email, phone, linkedin, notes, last_contacted, organisation_id, relationship_owner_id) - do not widen it.
expectedBehavior: admin/assessor PATCH updates only supplied fields and returns 200; omitted fields preserved on subsequent GET
viewer PATCH -> 403; unauthenticated -> 401; unknown id -> 404
Fields outside the ContactUpdate allowlist are ignored, not persisted
verificationSteps: cd backend && pytest tests/test_contacts.py -k 'patch or update or rbac'

description: Add an 'Edit' control on ContactsPage (admin+assessor only) that opens a form pre-filled from the contact's current values and submits PATCH /api/contacts/{id} on save, updating the row in place without a reload. Cancel makes no API call. Surface API errors without mutating the row. Viewers see no Edit control.
expectedBehavior: ContactsPage shows Edit for admin/assessor and hides it for viewer
Edit opens a form pre-filled with the contact's current values
Saving calls api.patch('/contacts/{id}', ...) with the changed fields and updates the row on resolve; Cancel makes no api.patch call
A rejected patch leaves the row unchanged and shows an error

expectedBehavior: admin/assessor PATCH updates only supplied fields and returns 200; omitted fields preserved on subsequent GET
